var_functions.query_db:16.subject: checkpatch: remove reference to feature-removal-schedule.txt
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
checkpatch: remove reference to feature-removal-schedule.txt

In commit 9c0ece069b32 ("Get rid of Documentation/feature-removal.txt"),
Linus removes feature-removal-schedule.txt from Documentation, but there
is still some reference to this file.  So remove them.

Signed-off-by: Tao Ma <85dcca6eaef7f88f8513274f73363a9b5feab3ec@taobao.com>
Acked-by: Andy Whitcroft <b4658fdd7fbd645c2859b0e39d71018f32058d12@canonical.com>

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>
Reviewed-by: joyce.xue <e513a8cfffdd1761b5c8047b6b6cbe23f11326f2@huawei.com>

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>
Cc: Joel Becker <9d3d88cacb47f143c6cf36b9a61c09b528b2c49a@evilplan.org>
Cc: Mark Fasheh <8f0bc92cac940f3e83deb53ced7a1f201bce5732@suse.com>
Cc: Li Zefan <a8167b9d27aca9c6624754deb52c4c6420eded04@huawei.com>
Cc: "Duyongfeng (B)" <5a464628c2a5e0735f0b7683edbeb0e6ca1c2d8f@huawei.com>
Acked-by: Sunil Mushran <71202349b6bab84fd697c8fc05379fc0e6724c25@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Morton <5c1e68b099950c134891f0b6e179498a8ebe9cf9@linux-foundation.org>
Signed-off-by: Linus Torvalds <69652caca27c8b940640ad396ab71f93cacec34f@linux-foundation.org>

args.query:
SELECT 
    c.repo_name,
    c.message,
    LENGTH(c.message) as msg_length
FROM commits c
WHERE c.message IS NOT NULL 
    AND LENGTH(c.message) < 1000
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
LIMIT 10;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT 
    c.repo_name,
    c.message,
    LENGTH(c.message) as msg_length
FROM commits c
WHERE c.message IS NOT NULL 
    AND LENGTH(c.message) < 1000
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
LIMIT 10;

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

Add checks for Blackfin-specific issues that seem to crop up from time to
time.  In particular, we have helper macros to break a 32bit address into
the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

time.  In particular, we have helper macros to break a 32bit address into
the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.

Did you mean "pg_namespace"?

LINE 11:         FROM languages l

args.query:
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) as filtered_commit_count
FROM commits c
WHERE c.message IS NOT NULL 
    AND LENGTH(c.message) < 1000
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
    AND c.repo_name IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT l.repo_name
        FROM languages l
        INNER JOIN licenses li ON l.repo_name = li.repo_name
        WHERE l.language_description LIKE '%Shell%'
          AND li.license = 'apache-2.0'
    );

exec_args.sql:

    COUNT(*) as filtered_commit_count
FROM commits c
WHERE c.message IS NOT NULL 
    AND LENGTH(c.message) < 1000
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
    AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
    AND c.repo_name IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT l.repo_name
        FROM languages l
        INNER JOIN licenses li ON l.repo_name = li.repo_name
        WHERE l.language_description LIKE '%Shell%'
          AND li.license = 'apache-2.0'
    );

args.code:
# We need to read the repo list from the metadata_database first
# and then manually use it to filter commits on artifacts_database
import json

# This is the file path from the previous query on metadata_database
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']

with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    repo_data = json.load(f)

repo_names = [item['repo_name'] for item in repo_data]
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'message': f'Got {len(repo_names)} repos, creating IN clause',
    'sample': repo_names[:3]
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

val_args.code:
# We need to read the repo list from the metadata_database first
# and then manually use it to filter commits on artifacts_database
import json

# This is the file path from the previous query on metadata_database
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']

with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    repo_data = json.load(f)

repo_names = [item['repo_name'] for item in repo_data]
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'message': f'Got {len(repo_names)} repos, creating IN clause',
    'sample': repo_names[:3]
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

args.code:
import json

# Read the repo list file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    repo_data = json.load(f)

repo_names = [item['repo_name'] for item in repo_data]

# Since we can't pass all 114,972 repos at once, let's do this in batches
# First, let's verify that the repo names table exists in DuckDB
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_repos': len(repo_names),
    'first_batch_size': min(1000, len(repo_names)),
    'sample_repos': repo_names[:3]
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

import json

# Read the repo list file
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    repo_data = json.load(f)

repo_names = [item['repo_name'] for item in repo_data]

# Since we can't pass all 114,972 repos at once, let's do this in batches
# First, let's verify that the repo names table exists in DuckDB
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_repos': len(repo_names),
    'first_batch_size': min(1000, len(repo_names)),
    'sample_repos': repo_names[:3]
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

checkpatch: add some common Blackfin checks

Add checks for Blackfin-specific issues that seem to crop up from time to
time.  In particular, we have helper macros to break a 32bit address into
the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.

# Let's process this in batches using Python
# First, let's sample some repos and test the query
import json
import sqlite3

# Connect to metadata_database to get the repo list
metadata_db = "metadata_database"

# Let's use DuckDB's ability to attach SQLite
# We need to attach the SQLite database first

attachment_query = """
ATTACHEE 'metadata_database' AS meta (TYPE SQLITE);
"""

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'approach': 'Need to attach SQLite to DuckDB or query separately',
    'next_step': 'Query commits table with filters first, then join'
}))

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

import json

file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    repo_data = json.load(f)

repo_names = [item['repo_name'] for item in repo_data]
repo_count = len(repo_names)

# Create a string with all repo names for IN clause

escaped_repos = [f"'{repo.replace("'", "''")}'" for repo in repo_names]

    'total_repos': repo_count,

}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

checkpatch: add some common Blackfin checks

Add checks for Blackfin-specific issues that seem to crop up from time to
time.  In particular, we have helper macros to break a 32bit address into
the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.

", "msg_length": "283"}, {"repo_name": "torvalds/linux", "message": "shmem: recalculate file inode when fstat

Shmem uses shmem_recalc_inode to update i_blocks when it allocates page,
undoes range or swaps.  But mm can drop clean page without notifying
shmem.  This makes fstat sometimes return out-of-date block size.

The problem can be partially solved when we add
inode_operations->getattr which calls shmem_recalc_inode to update
i_blocks for fstat.

shmem_recalc_inode also updates counter used by statfs and
vm_committed_as.  For them the situation is not changed.  They still
suffer from the discrepancy after dropping clean page and before the
function is called by aforementioned triggers.

Signed-off-by: Yu Zhao <9640a31789aee25cf713d8aa6fe964eb05bab72b@google.com>
Signed-off-by: Hugh Dickins <d3abdf3e2800e6cb849f11b81e6eb50b34d96431@google.com>

Signed-off-by: Linus Torvalds <69652caca27c8b940640ad396ab71f93cacec34f@linux-foundation.org>

args.query:
SELECT repo_name, message, LENGTH(message) as msg_length
FROM commits
WHERE message IS NOT NULL 
  AND LENGTH(message) < 1000
  AND LOWER(message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
  AND LOWER(message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
  AND LOWER(message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
LIMIT 17976;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT repo_name, message, LENGTH(message) as msg_length
FROM commits
WHERE message IS NOT NULL 
  AND LENGTH(message) < 1000
  AND LOWER(message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
  AND LOWER(message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
  AND LOWER(message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
LIMIT 17976;

args.code:
import json
import sqlite3

# Read the repo list from the metadata_database SQLite file
# Let's directly connect to the SQLite database and check its structure
conn_sqlite = sqlite3.connect('metadata_database')
cursor = conn_sqlite.cursor()

# Check what tables exist
cursor.execute("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table';")
tables = cursor.fetchall()
print('SQLite tables:', tables)

# Check structure of languages and licenses tables
cursor.execute("PRAGMA table_info(languages);")
langs_schema = cursor.fetchall()
cursor.execute("PRAGMA table_info(licenses);")
lics_schema = cursor.fetchall()

conn_sqlite.close()

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'sqlite_tables': tables,
    'languages_schema': langs_schema,
    'licenses_schema': lics_schema
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

FROM languages l

WHERE l.repo_name = 'torvalds/linux';

exec_args.sql:

args.code:
import json

# Read the commits sample to understand the structure
commits_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:42']
with open(commits_file, 'r') as f:
    commits_data = json.load(f)

# Extract unique repos from commits
commit_repos = list(set([item['repo_name'] for item in commits_data]))

# Read the apache shell repos file
apache_shell_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(apache_shell_file, 'r') as f:
    apache_shell_data = json.load(f)

apache_shell_repos = [item['repo_name'] for item in apache_shell_data]

# Find overlap between the two sets
overlap = list(set(commit_repos) & set(apache_shell_repos))

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_commit_repos': len(commit_repos),
    'total_apache_shell_repos': len(apache_shell_repos),
    'overlap_count': len(overlap),
    'sample_commit_repos': commit_repos[:5],
    'sample_apache_shell_repos': apache_shell_repos[:5],
    'sample_overlap': overlap[:5] if overlap else []
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync

args.query:
SELECT c.repo_name, COUNT(*) as filtered_commit_count
FROM commits c
WHERE c.message IS NOT NULL 
  AND LENGTH(c.message) < 1000
  AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
  AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
  AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
  AND c.repo_name IN (
    'Microsoft/vscode', 'torvalds/linux', 'facebook/react', 
    'apple/swift', 'twbs/bootstrap', 'tensorflow/tensorflow'
  )
GROUP BY c.repo_name;

exec_args.sql:
SELECT c.repo_name, COUNT(*) as filtered_commit_count
FROM commits c
WHERE c.message IS NOT NULL 
  AND LENGTH(c.message) < 1000
  AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'merge%'
  AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'update%'
  AND LOWER(c.message) NOT LIKE 'test%'
  AND c.repo_name IN (
    'Microsoft/vscode', 'torvalds/linux', 'facebook/react', 
    'apple/swift', 'twbs/bootstrap', 'tensorflow/tensorflow'
  )
GROUP BY c.repo_name;

args.code:
import json

# Read the apache shell repos file
apache_shell_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(apache_shell_file, 'r') as f:
    apache_shell_data = json.load(f)

apache_shell_repos = set(item['repo_name'] for item in apache_shell_data)

# Check which of the commit repos are in apache_shell_repos
commit_repos = ['Microsoft/vscode', 'torvalds/linux', 'facebook/react', 'apple/swift', 'twbs/bootstrap', 'tensorflow/tensorflow']

matching_repos = [repo for repo in commit_repos if repo in apache_shell_repos]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'commit_repos': commit_repos,
    'apache_shell_repos_count': len(apache_shell_repos),
    'matching_repos': matching_repos,
    'matching_count': len(matching_repos)
}))

import json

# Read the apache shell repos file
apache_shell_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:18']
with open(apache_shell_file, 'r') as f:
    apache_shell_data = json.load(f)

apache_shell_repos = set(item['repo_name'] for item in apache_shell_data)

# Check which of the commit repos are in apache_shell_repos
commit_repos = ['Microsoft/vscode', 'torvalds/linux', 'facebook/react', 'apple/swift', 'twbs/bootstrap', 'tensorflow/tensorflow']

matching_repos = [repo for repo in commit_repos if repo in apache_shell_repos]

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'commit_repos': commit_repos,
    'apache_shell_repos_count': len(apache_shell_repos),
    'matching_repos': matching_repos,
    'matching_count': len(matching_repos)
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

args.code:
import json

# Get the filtered commit counts from the query result
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    commit_counts = json.load(f)

# Filter for repos that match our apache shell criteria
# From previous analysis, these are: apple/swift and tensorflow/tensorflow
apache_shell_repos = ["apple/swift", "tensorflow/tensorflow"]

total_commits = 0
for item in commit_counts:
    if item['repo_name'] in apache_shell_repos:
        total_commits += int(item['filtered_commit_count'])

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_commit_messages_matching_criteria': total_commits,
    'breakdown': {item['repo_name']: item['filtered_commit_count'] for item in commit_counts if item['repo_name'] in apache_shell_repos}
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync

val_args.code:
import json

# Get the filtered commit counts from the query result
file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:52']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    commit_counts = json.load(f)

# Filter for repos that match our apache shell criteria
# From previous analysis, these are: apple/swift and tensorflow/tensorflow
apache_shell_repos = ["apple/swift", "tensorflow/tensorflow"]

total_commits = 0
for item in commit_counts:
    if item['repo_name'] in apache_shell_repos:
        total_commits += int(item['filtered_commit_count'])

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'total_commit_messages_matching_criteria': total_commits,
    'breakdown': {item['repo_name']: item['filtered_commit_count'] for item in commit_counts if item['repo_name'] in apache_shell_repos}
}))

var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: call o2quo_exit() if malloc failed in o2net_init()

In o2net_init, if malloc failed, it directly returns -ENOMEM.  Then
o2quo_exit won't be called in init_o2nm.

var_functions.query_db:16.subject: ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed
var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then
ocfs2_inode_lock().

But if ocfs2_inode_lock() failed, it goes to out_sems without unlocking
rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

Signed-off-by: Joseph Qi <88260f35dd98758635dbbf2145383e817a86f01c@huawei.com>

checkpatch: add some common Blackfin checks

Add checks for Blackfin-specific issues that seem to crop up from time to
time.  In particular, we have helper macros to break a 32bit address into
the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.

var_functions.query_db:16.message:
ocfs2: unlock rw lock if inode lock failed

In ocfs2_file_aio_write(), it does ocfs2_rw_lock() first and then

rw lock.  This will cause a bug in ocfs2_lock_res_free() when testing
res->l_ex_holders, which is increased in __ocfs2_cluster_lock() and
decreased in __ocfs2_cluster_unlock().

checkpatch: add some common Blackfin checks

Add checks for Blackfin-specific issues that seem to crop up from time to
time.  In particular, we have helper macros to break a 32bit address into
the hi/lo parts, and we want to make sure people use the csync/ssync
variant that includes fun anomaly workarounds.
